1	\chapter{Coherence of locally trivial line bundles}
2	\label{chap:Picard_LineBundleCoherence}
3	% archon:covers AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/LineBundleCoherence.lean
4	
5	% STRATEGY NOTE. This chapter supplies the cheap, on-path half of the
6	% line-bundle coherence requirement of the A.2.c Quot-scheme embedding
7	% (\cref{chap:Picard_QuotScheme}, \cref{chap:Picard_FGAPicRepresentability}).
8	% The Quot functor takes coherent quotients of a coherent sheaf; to feed it a
9	% line bundle one must know that the line bundle is itself coherent, i.e. a
10	% finitely presented \(\mathcal{O}\)-module. The embedding's input is a point of
11	% the relative Picard scheme \(\Pic^0_{C/k}\), whose carrier is the
12	% \emph{locally trivial} predicate \(\texttt{IsLocallyTrivial}\) of
13	% \cref{chap:Picard_LineBundlePullback} (the project-side stand-in for an
14	% invertible sheaf, a sheaf of \(\mathcal{O}\)-modules locally isomorphic to
15	% \(\mathcal{O}\) of rank one). The chapter proves
16	% \(\texttt{IsLocallyTrivial} M \Rightarrow M.\mathtt{IsFinitePresentation}\),
17	% together with a rank-one / flatness record. The general implication
18	% ``\(M\) tensor-invertible \(\Rightarrow\) \(M\) locally free of rank one''
19	% (the converse spreading-out, Stacks Lemma~0B8M when stalks are local) is
20	% \emph{not} crossed here: the carrier is already locally trivial, so the
21	% finite-presentation spreading-out is never needed.
22	
23	\section{Setup and motivation}
24	\label{sec:lbc_setup}
25	
26	Fix a scheme \(X\) and write \(X.\mathtt{Modules}\) for the category of sheaves
27	of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules (Mathlib's \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules}\) on the
28	ringed-space site of \(X\), with structure sheaf \(X.\mathtt{ringCatSheaf}\)).
29	A \emph{line bundle} on \(X\) is an invertible \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module; in the
30	sense of \cref{chap:Picard_LineBundlePullback} we model line bundles by the
31	predicate \(\texttt{IsLocallyTrivial}\): a module \(M : X.\mathtt{Modules}\) is locally
32	trivial of rank one if every point \(x \in X\) has an \emph{affine} open
33	neighbourhood \(U\) on which the restriction \(M|_U\) is isomorphic, as an
34	\(\mathcal{O}_U\)-module, to the structure sheaf-as-module \(\struct{U}\):
35	\[
36	  \texttt{IsLocallyTrivial}(M) \;:=\;
37	  \forall x \in X,\ \exists\, U \ni x \text{ affine open},\quad
38	  M|_U \;\cong\; \struct{U}.
39	\]
40	This is the rank-one case of Stacks Definition~17.25.1
41	(Tag~01CS, invertible \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules), and
42	agrees with the classical ``locally free of rank one'' description by Stacks
43	Lemma~17.25.4 (Tag~0B8M).
44	
45	The goal of the chapter is to record that such a module is \emph{coherent} in the
46	sense the Quot construction consumes, namely finitely presented:
47	\(M.\mathtt{IsFinitePresentation}\) in Mathlib's
48	\(\mathtt{SheafOfModules.IsFinitePresentation}\) (built from a
49	\(\mathtt{QuasicoherentData}\)). The mathematics is elementary: on each
50	trivialising chart \(M|_U \cong \struct{U}\) is finite free of rank one, hence
51	has the trivial finite presentation, and finite presentation is by definition a
52	local condition. No stalk computation and no spreading-out are involved. The
53	content is the bookkeeping that turns the pointwise trivialisation into the
54	indexed \(\mathtt{QuasicoherentData}\) Mathlib expects.
55	
56	\section{Site instances for quasi-coherent data}
57	\label{sec:lbc_site_instances}
58	
59	% NOTE (chief unknown). Mathlib's SheafOfModules.IsFinitePresentation /
60	% QuasicoherentData are stated under three site hypotheses on the slice
61	% topologies J.over X of the underlying site J of X.ringCatSheaf:
62	%   [∀ x, (J.over x).HasSheafCompose (forget₂ RingCat AddCommGrpCat)]
63	%   [∀ x, HasWeakSheafify (J.over x) AddCommGrpCat]
64	%   [∀ x, (J.over x).WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat]
65	% These must be discharged (or assumed as instances) before the statements in
66	% this chapter typecheck for X.ringCatSheaf. The project already lives in the
67	% SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf world, so these should resolve; this is the one
68	% item to verify when the Lean file is scaffolded.
69	
70	The Mathlib predicates \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules.IsFinitePresentation}\) and
71	\(\mathtt{SheafOfModules.QuasicoherentData}\) are stated relative to a
72	Grothendieck topology \(J\) on a base category \(C\) and a sheaf of rings
73	\(R : \Sheaf(J, \RingCat)\). They require, for every object \(x : C\), three
74	instances on the slice topology \(J/x\) (Mathlib \(J.\mathtt{over}\ x\)):
75	\[
76	  (J/x).\mathtt{HasSheafCompose}(\forget_{\RingCat \to \AddCommGrpCat}),\quad
77	  \HasWeakSheafify(J/x, \AddCommGrpCat),\quad
78	  (J/x).\mathtt{WEqualsLocallyBijective}(\AddCommGrpCat).
79	\]
80	For \(X.\mathtt{Modules} = \mathtt{SheafOfModules}\ X.\mathtt{ringCatSheaf}\)
81	these are the ambient site instances of the ringed space underlying \(X\). We
82	record their availability as a standing assumption for the chapter; verifying it
83	for \(X.\mathtt{ringCatSheaf}\) is the single instance-resolution check the
84	formalisation must clear at entry, and is independent of the mathematical
85	content below.
86	
87	\begin{remark}
88	  \label{rem:lbc_site_instances}
89	  For the ringed-space site underlying \(X\) with sheaf of rings
90	  \(R = X.\mathtt{ringCatSheaf}\), every slice topology \(J/x\) carries the
91	  three instances \(\mathtt{HasSheafCompose}\), \(\HasWeakSheafify\) and
92	  \(\mathtt{WEqualsLocallyBijective}\) (for the forgetful functor
93	  \(\RingCat \to \AddCommGrpCat\)) demanded by
94	  \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules.QuasicoherentData}\). Under this assumption the
95	  predicate \(M.\mathtt{IsFinitePresentation}\) is well-formed for every
96	  \(M : X.\mathtt{Modules}\).
97	\end{remark}
98	
99	\section{From pointwise triviality to a trivialising cover}
100	\label{sec:lbc_cover}
101	
102	\begin{lemma}[Trivialising affine cover]
103	  \label{lem:lbc_trivializing_cover}
104	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial.exists_trivializing_cover}
105	  \uses{def:line_bundle_on_product}
106	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Modules on Ringed Spaces, Definition 17.25.1
107	  % "invertible module" (Tag 01CS), Section 17.25 (Tag 01CR)
108	  % (read from references/stacks-modules.tex, L4046-L4059).
109	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
110	  % "Let $(X, \mathcal{O}_X)$ be a ringed space. An
111	  %  {\it invertible $\mathcal{O}_X$-module} is a sheaf
112	  %  of $\mathcal{O}_X$-modules $\mathcal{L}$ such that
113	  %  the functor
114	  %  $$
115	  %  \textit{Mod}(\mathcal{O}_X) \longrightarrow \textit{Mod}(\mathcal{O}_X),\quad
116	  %  \mathcal{F} \longmapsto \mathcal{L} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X} \mathcal{F}
117	  %  $$
118	  %  is an equivalence of categories. We say that $\mathcal{L}$ is
119	  %  {\it trivial} if it is isomorphic as an $\mathcal{O}_X$-module
120	  %  to $\mathcal{O}_X$."
121	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], Tag~01CS (Modules, Definition~17.25.1).}
122	  Let \(M : X.\mathtt{Modules}\) be locally trivial, \(\texttt{IsLocallyTrivial}(M)\).
123	  Then there exist an index set \(I\), a family of affine open subschemes
124	  \(\{U_i\}_{i \in I}\) of \(X\) covering \(X\) (i.e. \(\bigcup_i U_i = X\)), and
125	  for each \(i\) an isomorphism of \(\mathcal{O}_{U_i}\)-modules
126	  \[
127	    e_i \;:\; M|_{U_i} \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\; \struct{U_i}
128	  \]
129	  to the structure sheaf-as-module on \(U_i\).
130	\end{lemma}
131	
132	\begin{proof}
133	  \uses{def:line_bundle_on_product}
134	  Apply the hypothesis \(\texttt{IsLocallyTrivial}(M)\) at every point \(x \in X\): it
135	  produces an affine open \(U_x \ni x\) and an isomorphism
136	  \(e_x : M|_{U_x} \cong \struct{U_x}\). Take the index set \(I := X\) (the
137	  underlying set of points), and to the index \(x\) assign the chosen
138	  neighbourhood \(U_x\) and the chosen isomorphism \(e_x\). The family
139	  \(\{U_x\}_{x \in X}\) covers \(X\) because each \(x\) lies in its own \(U_x\).
140	  This is exactly the data the definition supplies, packaged as an indexed
141	  family rather than a pointwise existential.
142	\end{proof}
143	
144	\section{The trivial presentation on a chart}
145	\label{sec:lbc_chart_presentation}
146	
147	\begin{lemma}[Finite free presentation on a chart]
148	  \label{lem:lbc_chart_presentation}
149	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial.chartPresentation}
150	  \uses{lem:lbc_trivializing_cover}
151	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Modules on Ringed Spaces, section
152	  % "Modules of finite presentation" (\label{definition-finite-presentation})
153	  % (read from references/stacks-modules.tex, L1377-L1392).
154	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
155	  % "We say that $\mathcal{F}$ is of {\it finite presentation}
156	  %  if for every point $x \in X$ there exists an open neighbourhood
157	  %  $x\in U \subset X$, and  $n, m \in \mathbf{N}$ such that $\mathcal{F}|_U$
158	  %  is isomorphic to the cokernel of a map
159	  %  $$
160	  %  \bigoplus\nolimits_{j = 1, \ldots, m}
161	  %  \mathcal{O}_U
162	  %  \longrightarrow
163	  %  \bigoplus\nolimits_{i = 1, \ldots, n}
164	  %  \mathcal{O}_U
165	  %  $$"
166	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], Modules, ``Modules of finite
167	  presentation''.}
168	  On each chart \(U_i\) of the trivialising cover of
169	  \cref{lem:lbc_trivializing_cover}, the restriction \(M|_{U_i}\) admits the
170	  trivial finite free presentation
171	  \[
172	    0 \;\longrightarrow\; \struct{U_i} \;\longrightarrow\; M|_{U_i}
173	    \;\longrightarrow\; 0,
174	  \]
175	  i.e. \(M|_{U_i}\) is the cokernel of the zero map
176	  \(\bigoplus_{j \in \varnothing} \struct{U_i} \to \struct{U_i}\)
177	  (the case \(m = 0\), \(n = 1\) of the finite-presentation pattern). The
178	  unit sheaf-as-module \(\struct{U_i}\) (Mathlib's \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules.unit}\))
179	  carries a canonical free presentation on one generator with no relations;
180	  this presentation is finite.
181	\end{lemma}
182	
183	\begin{proof}
184	  \uses{lem:lbc_trivializing_cover}
185	  The structure sheaf-as-module \(\struct{U_i}\) is free of rank one on the
186	  single generator \(1\): the identity map
187	  \(\struct{U_i} \to \struct{U_i}\) exhibits it as
188	  \(\bigoplus_{i \in \{*\}} \struct{U_i}\), so the surjection
189	  \(\struct{U_i}^{\oplus 1} \twoheadrightarrow \struct{U_i}\) is an isomorphism
190	  and its kernel is zero, generated by the empty family. Thus
191	  \(\struct{U_i}\) has a presentation with \(n = 1\) generators and \(m = 0\)
192	  relations; this presentation is finite. Transporting it along the
193	  trivialisation \(e_i : M|_{U_i} \xrightarrow{\sim} \struct{U_i}\) of
194	  \cref{lem:lbc_trivializing_cover} (presentations are stable under
195	  isomorphism of the presented module) yields a finite free presentation of
196	  \(M|_{U_i}\).
197	\end{proof}
198	
199	\section{Finite presentation of a locally trivial line bundle}
200	\label{sec:lbc_finite_presentation}
201	
202	\begin{theorem}[Locally trivial \(\Rightarrow\) finitely presented]
203	  \label{thm:lbc_isFinitePresentation}
204	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial.isFinitePresentation}
205	  \uses{lem:lbc_trivializing_cover, lem:lbc_chart_presentation, rem:lbc_site_instances}
206	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Modules on Ringed Spaces,
207	  % Lemma 17.25.4 (Tag 0B8M), invertible vs locally free of rank one
208	  % (read from references/stacks-modules.tex, L4159-L4165).
209	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
210	  % "Let $(X, \mathcal{O}_X)$ be a ringed space. Any locally free
211	  %  $\mathcal{O}_X$-module of rank $1$ is invertible.
212	  %  If all stalks $\mathcal{O}_{X, x}$ are local rings, then
213	  %  the converse holds as well (but in general this is not the case)."
214	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], Tag~0B8M (Modules, Lemma~17.25.4).}
215	  Let \(X\) be a scheme satisfying \cref{rem:lbc_site_instances} and let
216	  \(M : X.\mathtt{Modules}\) be locally trivial, \(\texttt{IsLocallyTrivial}(M)\). Then
217	  \(M\) is finitely presented: \(M.\mathtt{IsFinitePresentation}\) holds in
218	  Mathlib's \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules.IsFinitePresentation}\).
219	\end{theorem}
220	
221	\begin{proof}
222	  \uses{lem:lbc_trivializing_cover, lem:lbc_chart_presentation}
223	  By \cref{lem:lbc_trivializing_cover} choose a trivialising affine cover
224	  \(\{U_i\}_{i \in I}\) with isomorphisms \(e_i : M|_{U_i} \cong \struct{U_i}\).
225	  Assemble these into a \(\mathtt{QuasicoherentData}\) for \(M\): the index type
226	  is \(I\), the covering objects are the affine opens \(U_i\) (which cover \(X\)),
227	  and the presentation attached to index \(i\) is the finite free presentation of
228	  \(M|_{U_i}\) (equivalently, of the restriction \(M.\mathtt{over}\,U_i\))
229	  produced in \cref{lem:lbc_chart_presentation}. Each such presentation is
230	  finite, so the resulting quasi-coherent datum is a
231	  \(\mathtt{QuasicoherentData.IsFinitePresentation}\). Feeding it to
232	  \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules.IsFinitePresentation.mk}\) gives
233	  \(M.\mathtt{IsFinitePresentation}\).
234	
235	  The only non-formal input is the trivialising cover; everything else is the
236	  definitional unwinding of finite presentation as a local property
237	  (Stacks, ``Modules of finite presentation''), since a rank-one free module is its own finite
238	  presentation. No stalk computation and no neighbourhood-spreading argument is
239	  used: the hypothesis already provides the local free models.
240	\end{proof}
241	
242	\begin{corollary}[Locally trivial \(\Rightarrow\) finite type and quasi-coherent]
243	  \label{cor:lbc_isFiniteType}
244	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial.isFiniteType}
245	  \uses{thm:lbc_isFinitePresentation}
246	  Under the hypotheses of \cref{thm:lbc_isFinitePresentation}, the locally
247	  trivial module \(M\) is also of finite type
248	  (\(M.\mathtt{IsFiniteType}\)) and quasi-coherent
249	  (\(M.\mathtt{IsQuasicoherent}\)).
250	\end{corollary}
251	
252	\begin{proof}
253	  \uses{thm:lbc_isFinitePresentation}
254	  Finite presentation implies finite type by Mathlib's
255	  \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules.instIsFiniteTypeOfIsFinitePresentation}\), and implies
256	  quasi-coherence by
257	  \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules.instIsQuasicoherentOfIsFinitePresentation}\); both are
258	  immediate from \cref{thm:lbc_isFinitePresentation}.
259	\end{proof}
260	
261	\section{Rank one and flatness record}
262	\label{sec:lbc_rank_flat}
263	
264	% NOTE. Mathlib (at the pin) has no SheafOfModules-level "locally free of rank r"
265	% or "flat" predicate (verified absent: no SheafOfModules.IsLocallyFree). The
266	% rank-one and flatness facts are therefore recorded chart-locally, as the data
267	% the Quot embedding actually consumes, rather than as instances of a missing
268	% global predicate. Project-side predicates may be introduced if a downstream
269	% consumer needs them packaged; this is flagged for the plan agent.
270	
271	\begin{lemma}[Chart-local rank one and flatness]
272	  \label{lem:lbc_rank_flat}
273	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial.chart_free_rank_one}
274	  \uses{lem:lbc_trivializing_cover}
275	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Modules on Ringed Spaces, section
276	  % "Locally free sheaves" (\label{definition-locally-free}, "(finite)
277	  % locally free of rank $r$")
278	  % (read from references/stacks-modules.tex, L2079-L2094).
279	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
280	  % "\item We say $\mathcal{F}$ is {\it locally free} if for every
281	  %  point $x \in X$ there exist a set $I$ and an open
282	  %  neighbourhood $x \in U \subset X$
283	  %  such that $\mathcal{F}|_U$ is isomorphic to
284	  %  $\bigoplus_{i \in I} \mathcal{O}_X|_U$ as an $\mathcal{O}_X|_U$-module.
285	  %  \item We say $\mathcal{F}$ is {\it finite locally free} if we may
286	  %  choose the index sets $I$ to be finite.
287	  %  \item We say $\mathcal{F}$ is {\it finite locally free of rank $r$}
288	  %  if we may choose the index sets $I$ to have cardinality $r$."
289	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], Modules, ``Locally free sheaves'' (finite
290	  locally free of rank \(r\)).}
291	  Let \(M : X.\mathtt{Modules}\) be locally trivial. On each chart \(U_i\) of the
292	  trivialising cover of \cref{lem:lbc_trivializing_cover} the restriction
293	  \(M|_{U_i} \cong \struct{U_i}\) is free of rank one over
294	  \(\mathcal{O}_{U_i}\); in particular it is flat, because \(\struct{U_i}\) is a
295	  flat \(\mathcal{O}_{U_i}\)-module (a ring is a flat module over itself, and
296	  flatness is preserved by the isomorphism \(e_i\)). Consequently \(M\) is, in
297	  the sense of \cref{def:line_bundle_on_product}, finite locally free of rank one
298	  and flat: these properties hold on the cover \(\{U_i\}\) by construction.
299	\end{lemma}
300	
301	\begin{proof}
302	  \uses{lem:lbc_trivializing_cover}
303	  Immediate from the trivialisation: on \(U_i\), \(e_i\) identifies \(M|_{U_i}\)
304	  with \(\struct{U_i} = \bigoplus_{i \in \{*\}} \struct{U_i}\), the free module
305	  of rank one. The structure sheaf-as-module is flat over itself, and an
306	  isomorphic module is flat; the index set \(\{*\}\) has cardinality one, so the
307	  rank is one. The properties are stated on the chart \(U_i\) because there is no
308	  \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules}\)-level locally free or flat predicate globally; the chart-local statements are
309	  what the Quot-scheme embedding consumes when it forms the Hilbert-polynomial
310	  input on a fibre.
311	\end{proof}

8	/-!
9	# Line-bundle pullback on a relative curve (A.1.b)
10	
11	This file is the **A.1.b** file-skeleton sub-build chapter for the project's
12	positive-genus arm of `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It packages the line-bundle
13	pullback functor `π_T^* : Pic(T) → Pic(C ×_S T)` along the projection
14	`π_T : C ×_S T → T` of a relative curve, and the set-valued relative Picard
15	presheaf
16	
17	```
18	Pic^♯_{C/S}(T) := Pic(C ×_S T) / π_T^* Pic(T)
19	```
20	
21	as a functor on `(Sch/S)^op`.
22	
23	## Status (iter-174 Lane E file-skeleton)
24	
25	This file is the **iter-174 Lane E** file-skeleton: each of the five pinned
26	declarations carries the *intended* substantive type signature (matching the
27	blueprint `\lean{...}` pin in `chapters/Picard_LineBundlePullback.tex`) with a
28	`sorry` body. The bodies are iter-175+ work after the sibling chapters
29	`Picard_RelativeSpec.lean` (A.1.a) settles its `QcohAlgebra` body, and after
30	`Picard_RelPicFunctor.lean` (A.1.c) lands the étale-sheafification overlay.

48	## Note on type expressivity
49	
50	Because Mathlib `b80f227` ships no `Module.Invertible`/`IsInvertible` predicate
51	on `Scheme.Modules` (the closest is `Module.Invertible R M` for `R : CommRing`),
52	the type `OnProduct` packaging *line bundles* (= invertible sheaves of
53	`O`-modules) on the product cannot yet be carved out of `Scheme.Modules` as a
54	subtype. Following the project rule "Never weaken the type to dodge the proof"
55	we encode `OnProduct` by a typed `sorry` at the type level: the iter-175+ body
56	will instantiate it as a structure pairing a `(pullback πC πT).Modules` carrier
57	with an `IsInvertible` witness once that predicate is in Mathlib (or proven
58	internally as the project-side definition).
59	
60	The 4 pinned theorems/defs reference `OnProduct` as a typed-`sorry` carrier;
61	their signatures are still substantive (each declares a non-tautological claim:
62	a map between line-bundle types, a transitivity-of-pullback equality, an
63	equivalence relation on line bundles, an induced quotient map) and the bodies
64	collapse once `OnProduct` is unpacked.
65	
66	## References
67	
68	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_LineBundlePullback.tex` (444 LOC,
69	5 pins). Source: [Kleiman], "The Picard scheme", §2 (FGA Explained Ch.9 §9.2),
70	Definitions `df:aPf` (absolute Picard functor) and `df:Pfs` (relative Picard
71	functor). Stacks Project tags 01HG (pullback preserves invertibility),
72	01HH (functoriality of pullback), 01HK (invertible modules), 01CR
73	(Picard group of a scheme).
74	-/
75	
76	set_option autoImplicit false
77	
78	universe u
79	
80	open CategoryTheory Limits
81	
82	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
83	
84	namespace Scheme
85	
86	namespace LineBundle
87	
88	/-! ## §1. Line bundles on a product
89	
90	A *line bundle* on the relative curve `C ×_S T` is an invertible
91	`O_(C ×_S T)`-module, i.e. a sheaf of modules locally free of rank one. In
92	Mathlib `b80f227` the `Scheme.Modules` category is in place
93	(`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.Sheaf`), but there is no `IsInvertible`
94	predicate on its objects yet (the closest is `Module.Invertible R M` for rings,
95	in `Mathlib.RingTheory.PicardGroup`). iter-187 supplies the missing
96	predicate as a project-side definition via local trivialisation on an affine
97	open cover (see `IsLocallyTrivial` below), and refines `OnProduct` to the
98	corresponding subtype.
99	
100	Blueprint reference: `def:line_bundle_on_product` (Kleiman §2, "absolute Picard
101	functor"; Stacks tag 01HK). -/
102	
103	/-- A sheaf of `𝒪_X`-modules `M` is **locally trivial of rank one** if every
104	point `x : X` has an affine open neighbourhood `U` on which the restriction
105	`M|_U` is isomorphic to the structure sheaf-as-module `𝒪_U`. This is the
106	project-side stand-in for the missing Mathlib `IsInvertible` predicate on
107	`Scheme.Modules`: line bundles (invertible sheaves) are exactly the
108	`IsLocallyTrivial` modules, by Stacks tag 01HK / Hartshorne II §6. Local
109	trivialisability is the structural content that survives base change along
110	`f : Y ⟶ X` (Stacks 01HH) and is the source of the invertibility-preservation
111	step for `pullbackAlongProjection`.
112	
113	The witness is bundled as `Nonempty (M.restrict U.ι ≅ SheafOfModules.unit _)`:
114	the existence of a sheaf-of-modules isomorphism over the affine chart. -/
115	def IsLocallyTrivial {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) : Prop :=
116	  ∀ x : X, ∃ U : X.Opens, x ∈ U ∧ IsAffineOpen U ∧
117	    Nonempty (M.restrict U.ι ≅ SheafOfModules.unit (U : Scheme).ringCatSheaf)
118	
119	/-- The type of **line bundles on the product** `C ×_S T`: invertible sheaves of
120	`O_(C ×_S T)`-modules, where the product is the categorical pullback of the
121	projections `πC : C ⟶ S` and `πT : T ⟶ S`. In Kleiman's notation with `X = C`
122	and base `S`, this is `Pic_C(T) = Pic(C ×_S T)`, the value of the absolute
123	Picard functor at `T`.
124	
125	iter-187: refined to the subtype of `(Limits.pullback πC πT).Modules` cut out
126	by the project-side predicate `IsLocallyTrivial` (locally trivial of rank one).
127	The carrier is now genuinely the line-bundle subobject of `Scheme.Modules`,
128	matching the blueprint statement `def:line_bundle_on_product`. Iter-186's
129	all-modules carrier is recovered by forgetting the second component. -/
130	def OnProduct {S C T : Scheme.{u}} (πC : C ⟶ S) (πT : T ⟶ S) : Type (u+1) :=
131	  { M : (Limits.pullback πC πT).Modules // IsLocallyTrivial M }
132	
133	/-- Projection from a line bundle on the product to its underlying
134	sheaf of modules. -/
135	abbrev OnProduct.carrier {S C T : Scheme.{u}} {πC : C ⟶ S} {πT : T ⟶ S}
136	    (L : OnProduct πC πT) : (Limits.pullback πC πT).Modules := L.1
137	
138	/-- A line bundle on the product is locally trivial of rank one. -/
139	lemma OnProduct.isLocallyTrivial {S C T : Scheme.{u}} {πC : C ⟶ S} {πT : T ⟶ S}
140	    (L : OnProduct πC πT) : IsLocallyTrivial L.carrier := L.2
141	
142	/-- **Stacks 01HH (pullback of an invertible sheaf is invertible).** For any
143	morphism of schemes `f : Y ⟶ X` and any locally-trivial sheaf of `𝒪_X`-modules
144	`M`, the pulled-back module `f^* M : Y.Modules` is again locally trivial.
145	
146	The proof unfolds an affine open cover trivialising `M`, pulls back along `f`,
147	and observes that `f^*` commutes with restriction along open immersions (the
148	`pullback`/`restrictFunctor` natural isomorphism in
149	`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.Sheaf`) so that the trivialising chart for
150	`M|_U` becomes a trivialising chart for `(f^* M)|_(f⁻¹ U)`. The full chart-chase
151	involves the natural-isomorphism `restrictFunctorIsoPullback` plus the
152	preservation of `SheafOfModules.unit` under pullback (Mathlib
153	`Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Sheaf.PullbackFree`'s
154	`unitToPushforwardObjUnit`). Iter-187 wraps this chase as a named typed sorry
155	(helper-budget = 1); iter-188+ instantiates the chart-chase. -/
156	lemma IsLocallyTrivial.pullback {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X) {M : X.Modules}
157	    (hM : IsLocallyTrivial M) :
158	    IsLocallyTrivial ((Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj M) := by
159	  intro y
160	  obtain ⟨U, hxU, hU_aff, eM⟩ := hM (f.base y)
161	  have hyU' : y ∈ f ⁻¹ᵁ U := hxU
162	  obtain ⟨V, hV_aff, hyV, hVU⟩ := exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset hyU'
163	  refine ⟨V, hyV, hV_aff, ?_⟩
164	  -- Stacks 01HH chart-chase: factor `V.ι ≫ f` through `U` using
165	  -- `Scheme.Hom.resLE`, then transport through `restrictFunctorIsoPullback`
166	  -- and `pullbackComp` to identify the pullback at `V` with the pullback of
167	  -- the unit on `U` to `V`. Close via `eM` and `pullbackObjUnitToUnit`
168	  -- (which is an iso since `Opens.map g.base` is final via the
169	  -- `RepresentablyFlat` instance).
170	  obtain ⟨eM⟩ := eM

6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Sheaf.Generators
9	public import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Sheaf.Abelian
10	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Comma.Over.Pullback

12	/-!
13	# Quasicoherent sheaves
14	
15	A sheaf of modules is quasi-coherent if it admits locally a presentation as the
16	cokernel of a morphism between coproducts of copies of the sheaf of rings.
17	When these coproducts are finite, we say that the sheaf is of finite presentation.
18	
19	## References
20	
21	* https://stacks.math.columbia.edu/tag/01BD
22	
23	-/
24	
25	@[expose] public section
26	
27	universe w u v₁ v₂ u₁ u₂
28	
29	open CategoryTheory Limits
30	
31	variable {C : Type u₁} [Category.{v₁} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
32	  {R : Sheaf J RingCat.{u}}
33	
34	namespace SheafOfModules
35	
36	section
37	
38	variable [HasWeakSheafify J AddCommGrpCat.{u}] [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat.{u}]
39	  [J.HasSheafCompose (forget₂ RingCat.{u} AddCommGrpCat.{u})]
40	
41	/-- A global presentation of a sheaf of modules `M` consists of a family `generators.s`
42	of sections `s` which generate `M`, and a family of sections which generate
43	the kernel of the morphism `generators.π : free (generators.I) ⟶ M`. -/
44	structure Presentation (M : SheafOfModules.{u} R) where
45	  /-- generators -/
46	  generators : M.GeneratingSections
47	  /-- relations -/
48	  relations : (kernel generators.π).GeneratingSections
49	
50	/-- A global presentation of a sheaf of module if finite if the type
51	of generators and relations are finite. -/
52	class Presentation.IsFinite {M : SheafOfModules.{u} R} (p : M.Presentation) : Prop where
53	  isFiniteType_generators : p.generators.IsFiniteType := by infer_instance
54	  isFiniteType_relations : p.relations.IsFiniteType := by infer_instance
55	
56	attribute [instance] Presentation.IsFinite.isFiniteType_generators
57	  Presentation.IsFinite.isFiniteType_relations
58	
59	@[deprecated Presentation.IsFinite.isFiniteType_relations (since := "2026-04-14")]
60	lemma Presentation.IsFinite.finite_relations {M : SheafOfModules.{u} R} (p : M.Presentation)
61	    [p.IsFinite] : Finite p.relations.I := GeneratingSections.IsFiniteType.finite
62	
63	end
64	
65	noncomputable section
66	
67	variable {C : Type u₁} [Category.{v₁} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C} {R : Sheaf J RingCat.{u}}
68	  [HasSheafify J AddCommGrpCat] [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat]
69	  [J.HasSheafCompose (forget₂ RingCat AddCommGrpCat)] {ι σ : Type u}
70	
71	/-- Given two morphisms of sheaves of `R`-modules `f : free ι ⟶ free σ` and `g : free σ ⟶ M`
72	satisfying `H : f ≫ g = 0` and `IsColimit (CokernelCofork.ofπ g H)`, we obtain
73	generators of `Presentation M`. -/
74	@[simps! I s]
75	def generatorsOfIsCokernelFree {M : SheafOfModules.{u} R}
76	    (f : free ι ⟶ free σ) (g : free σ ⟶ M) (H : f ≫ g = 0)
77	    (H' : IsColimit (CokernelCofork.ofπ g H)) : M.GeneratingSections where
78	  I := σ
79	  s := M.freeHomEquiv g
80	  epi := by simpa using epi_of_isColimit_cofork H'
81	
82	@[simp]
83	theorem generatorsOfIsCokernelFree_π {M : SheafOfModules.{u} R}
84	    (f : free ι ⟶ free σ) (g : free σ ⟶ M) (H : f ≫ g = 0)
85	    (H' : IsColimit (CokernelCofork.ofπ g H)) :
86	    (generatorsOfIsCokernelFree f g H H').π = g := M.freeHomEquiv.symm_apply_apply g
87	
88	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
89	/-- Given two morphisms of sheaves of `R`-modules `f : free ι ⟶ free σ` and `g : free σ ⟶ M`
90	satisfying `H : f ≫ g = 0` and `IsColimit (CokernelCofork.ofπ g H)`, we obtain
91	relations of `Presentation M`. -/
92	@[simps! I s]
93	def relationsOfIsCokernelFree {M : SheafOfModules.{u} R}
94	    (f : free ι ⟶ free σ) (g : free σ ⟶ M) (H : f ≫ g = 0)
95	    (H' : IsColimit (CokernelCofork.ofπ g H)) :
96	    (kernel (generatorsOfIsCokernelFree f g H H').π).GeneratingSections where
97	  I := ι
98	  s := (kernel (generatorsOfIsCokernelFree f g H H').π).freeHomEquiv <| kernel.lift
99	    (generatorsOfIsCokernelFree f g H H').π f (by simp [H])
100	  epi := by
101	    let h : cokernel f ≅ M := (H'.coconePointUniqueUpToIso (colimit.isColimit _)).symm
102	    let h' : Abelian.image f ≅ kernel (generatorsOfIsCokernelFree f g H H').π :=
103	      kernel.mapIso (cokernel.π f) (generatorsOfIsCokernelFree f g H H').π
104	        (Iso.refl _) h (by simp [h])
105	    have comp_aux : Abelian.factorThruImage f ≫ h'.hom =
106	      (kernel.lift (generatorsOfIsCokernelFree f g H H').π f (by simp [H])) :=
107	        equalizer.hom_ext <| by simp [h']
108	    rw [← comp_aux, Equiv.symm_apply_apply]
109	    infer_instance
110	
111	/-- Given two morphisms of sheaves of `R`-modules `f : free ι ⟶ free σ` and `g : free σ ⟶ M`
112	satisfying `H : f ≫ g = 0` and `IsColimit (CokernelCofork.ofπ g H)`, we obtain a
113	`Presentation M`. -/
114	@[simps]
115	def presentationOfIsCokernelFree {M : SheafOfModules.{u} R}
116	    (f : free ι ⟶ free σ) (g : free σ ⟶ M) (H : f ≫ g = 0)
117	    (H' : IsColimit (CokernelCofork.ofπ g H)) : Presentation M where
118	  generators := generatorsOfIsCokernelFree f g H H'
119	  relations := relationsOfIsCokernelFree f g H H'
120	
121	/-- Given a sheaf of `R`-modules `M` and a `Presentation M`, there is two morphism of
122	sheaves of `R`-modules `f : free ι ⟶ free σ` and `g : free σ ⟶ M` satisfying `H : f ≫ g = 0`
123	and `IsColimit (CokernelCofork.ofπ g H)`. -/
124	def Presentation.isColimit {M : SheafOfModules.{u} R} (P : Presentation M) :
125	    IsColimit (CokernelCofork.ofπ (f := (freeHomEquiv _).symm P.relations.s ≫ (kernel.ι _))
126	      P.generators.π (by simp)) :=
127	  isCokernelEpiComp (c := CokernelCofork.ofπ _ (kernel.condition P.generators.π))
128	      (Abelian.epiIsCokernelOfKernel _ <| limit.isLimit _) _ rfl
129	
130	/-- Mapping a presentation under an isomorphism. -/
131	@[simps]
132	noncomputable def Presentation.ofIsIso {M N : SheafOfModules.{u} R} (f : M ⟶ N) [IsIso f]
133	    (σ : M.Presentation) : N.Presentation where
134	  generators := σ.generators.ofEpi f
135	  relations := σ.relations.ofEpi ((kernelCompMono _ f).symm.trans <| eqToIso (by simp)).hom
136	
137	@[deprecated (since := "2026-04-15")] alias Presentation.of_isIso := Presentation.ofIsIso
138	
139	instance {M N : SheafOfModules.{u} R} (f : M ⟶ N) [IsIso f]
140	    (σ : M.Presentation) [σ.IsFinite] : (σ.ofIsIso f).IsFinite where
141	  isFiniteType_generators := inferInstanceAs (σ.generators.ofEpi _).IsFiniteType
142	  isFiniteType_relations := inferInstanceAs (σ.relations.ofEpi _).IsFiniteType
143	
144	variable {C' : Type u₂} [Category.{v₂} C'] {J' : GrothendieckTopology C'} {S : Sheaf J' RingCat.{u}}
145	  [HasSheafify J' AddCommGrpCat] [J'.WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat]
146	  [J'.HasSheafCompose (forget₂ RingCat AddCommGrpCat)]
147	
148	variable {M : SheafOfModules.{u} R} (P : Presentation M)
149	  (F : SheafOfModules.{u} R ⥤ SheafOfModules.{u} S) [PreservesColimitsOfSize.{u, u} F]
150	  (η : F.obj (unit R) ≅ unit S)
151	
152	-- `preservesColimitsOfSize_shrink` is not a global instance because it loops indefinitely.
153	-- But here it is fine as an instance since the universe `u` is inferrable from the type of `F`.
154	local instance : PreservesColimitsOfSize.{0, 0} F := preservesColimitsOfSize_shrink _
155	
156	/-- Let `F` be a functor from sheaf of `R`-module to sheaf of `S`-module, if `F` preserves
157	colimits and `F.obj (unit R) ≅ unit S`, given a `P : Presentation M`, then we will obtain
158	relations of `Presentation (F.obj M)`. -/
159	def Presentation.mapRelations : free P.relations.I (R := S) ⟶ free P.generators.I :=
160	  (mapFree F η P.relations.I).inv ≫ F.map ((freeHomEquiv _).symm P.relations.s) ≫
161	    F.map (kernel.ι _) ≫ (mapFree F η P.generators.I).hom
162	
163	/-- Let `F` be a functor from sheaf of `R`-module to sheaf of `S`-module, if `F` preserves
164	colimits and `F.obj (unit R) ≅ unit S`, given a `P : Presentation M`, then we will obtain
165	generators of `Presentation (F.obj M)`. -/
166	def Presentation.mapGenerators : free P.generators.I ⟶ F.obj M :=
167	  (mapFree F η P.generators.I).inv ≫ F.map (P.generators.π)
168	
169	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
170	theorem Presentation.mapRelations_mapGenerators :
171	    P.mapRelations F η ≫ P.mapGenerators F η = 0 := by
172	  simp only [mapRelations, mapGenerators, Category.assoc, Iso.hom_inv_id_assoc,
173	    ← Functor.map_comp, kernel.condition, Functor.map_zero, comp_zero]
174	
175	/-- Let `F` be a functor from sheaf of `R`-module to sheaf of `S`-module, if `F` preserves
176	colimits and `F.obj (unit R) ≅ unit S`, given a `P : Presentation M`, then we will get a
177	`Presentation (F.obj M)`. -/
178	@[simps! generators_I relations_I]

367	      J.superset_covering ((Sieve.functorPushforward_ofObjects_le _ _ _).trans
368	      (Sieve.ofObjects_mono fun i' ↦ by aesop)) ((D i).coversTop (.mk g))
369	  presentation i :=

371	      (S := (R.over _).over _) (R := R.over _) (𝟙 _) (𝟙 _)
372	      (by ext : 2; exact R.1.map_id _) (by ext : 2; exact R.1.map_id _)
373	    (((D i.1).presentation i.2).map e.inverse (.refl _)).ofIsIso
374	      (e.fullyFaithfulFunctor.preimageIso
375	      (by exact e.counitIso.app ((M.over (X i.1)).over ((D i.1).X i.2)))).hom

def pseudofunctor :
    Pseudofunctor (LocallyDiscrete Scheme.{u}ᵒᵖ) (Adj Cat) :=
  LocallyDiscrete.mkPseudofunctor
    (fun X ↦ Adj.mk (Cat.of X.unop.Modules))
    (fun f ↦ .mk (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f.unop).toCat)
    (fun _ ↦ Adj.iso₂Mk (Cat.Hom.isoMk (pullbackId _))
        (Cat.Hom.isoMk (pushforwardId _).symm))
    (fun _ _ ↦ Adj.iso₂Mk (Cat.Hom.isoMk (pullbackComp _ _).symm)
        (Cat.Hom.isoMk (pushforwardComp _ _)))

end Functorial

but has better defeqs. -/

  letI α : X.presheaf ⟶ f.opensFunctor.op ⋙ Y.presheaf := { app U := (f.appIso U.unop).inv }
  SheafOfModules.pushforward (F := f.opensFunctor)
    ⟨Functor.whiskerRight α (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)⟩

/-- The restriction of a module along an open immersion. -/
abbrev restrict (M : Y.Modules) (f : X ⟶ Y) [IsOpenImmersion f] : X.Modules :=
  (restrictFunctor f).obj M

    Γ(M.restrict f, U) = Γ(M, f ''ᵁ U) := rfl

/-

Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
Authors: Christian Merten
-/
import AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.LineBundlePullback

/-!
# Coherence of locally trivial line bundles (A.2.c engine)

This file is the **iter-256 Lane engine** file-skeleton for the cheap, on-path
half of the line-bundle coherence requirement of the A.2.c Quot-scheme
embedding (`chap:Picard_QuotScheme`, `chap:Picard_FGAPicRepresentability`).

The Quot functor takes coherent quotients of a coherent sheaf; to feed it a line
bundle one must know that the line bundle is itself coherent, i.e. a finitely
presented `𝒪`-module. The embedding's input is a point of the relative Picard
scheme `Pic⁰_{C/k}`, whose carrier is the **locally trivial** predicate
`IsLocallyTrivial` of `chap:Picard_LineBundlePullback` (the project-side stand-in
for an invertible sheaf). The chapter proves
`IsLocallyTrivial M ⟹ M.IsFinitePresentation`, together with a rank-one /
flatness record. The general implication "`M` tensor-invertible `⟹` `M` locally
free of rank one" (the converse spreading-out, Stacks Lemma 0B8M when stalks are
local) is *not* crossed here: the carrier is already locally trivial, so the
finite-presentation spreading-out is never needed.

## Status (iter-256 file-skeleton)

This file is the **iter-256** file-skeleton: each of the five pinned declarations
carries the *intended* substantive type signature (matching the blueprint
`\lean{...}` pin in `chapters/Picard_LineBundleCoherence.tex`) with a `sorry`
body. The bodies are iter-257+ work, contingent on the site-instance de-risk
report below.

The 5 pinned declarations are:

1. `IsLocallyTrivial.exists_trivializing_cover` (`lem:lbc_trivializing_cover`) —
   repackage the pointwise existential of `IsLocallyTrivial` into an indexed
   trivialising affine cover.
2. `IsLocallyTrivial.chartPresentation` (`lem:lbc_chart_presentation`) — the
   trivial finite free presentation of `M.over U` on a trivialising chart.
3. `IsLocallyTrivial.isFinitePresentation` (`thm:lbc_isFinitePresentation`) —
   the main theorem: assemble a `QuasicoherentData` from the trivialising cover
   and feed it to `SheafOfModules.IsFinitePresentation.mk`.
4. `IsLocallyTrivial.isFiniteType` (`cor:lbc_isFiniteType`) — finite type +
   quasi-coherence, immediate from finite presentation.
5. `IsLocallyTrivial.chart_free_rank_one` (`lem:lbc_rank_flat`) — the chart-local
   rank-one free record.

## Site instances (the chief de-risk; see `rem:lbc_site_instances`)

Mathlib's `SheafOfModules.IsFinitePresentation` / `QuasicoherentData` are stated
under three site hypotheses on the slice topologies `J.over U` of the underlying
topology `J = Opens.grothendieckTopology X` of `X.ringCatSheaf`:

```
[∀ U, (J.over U).HasSheafCompose (forget₂ RingCat AddCommGrpCat)]
[∀ U, HasWeakSheafify (J.over U) AddCommGrpCat]     -- (resp. HasSheafify)
[∀ U, (J.over U).WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat]
```

The de-risk outcome (whether these resolve for `X.ringCatSheaf`) is recorded in
the task result and probed by the `#check` commands at the end of this file.

## References

Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_LineBundleCoherence.tex`.
Source: [Stacks Project], Modules on Ringed Spaces, Definition 17.25.1
(Tag 01CS, invertible modules), "Modules of finite presentation", and
Lemma 17.25.4 (Tag 0B8M).
-/

set_option autoImplicit false

universe u

open CategoryTheory Limits

namespace AlgebraicGeometry

namespace Scheme

namespace LineBundle

variable {X : Scheme.{u}}

/-! ## §1. From pointwise triviality to a trivialising cover -/

/-- **Trivialising affine cover** (Stacks 01CS / Definition 17.25.1).
A locally trivial module `M` admits an indexed trivialising affine cover: an
index type `I`, a family of affine opens `U i` covering `X`, and for each `i` a
`𝒪_{U_i}`-module isomorphism `M|_{U_i} ≅ 𝒪_{U_i}`.

The witness repackages the pointwise existential `IsLocallyTrivial M` (take
`I := X` and assign each point its chosen neighbourhood). -/
theorem IsLocallyTrivial.exists_trivializing_cover {M : X.Modules}
    (hM : IsLocallyTrivial M) :
    ∃ (I : Type u) (U : I → X.Opens),
      (∀ i, IsAffineOpen (U i)) ∧ iSup U = ⊤ ∧
        ∀ i, Nonempty (M.restrict (U i).ι ≅
          SheafOfModules.unit (U i : Scheme).ringCatSheaf) := by
  sorry

/-! ## §2. The trivial presentation on a chart -/

/-- **Finite free presentation on a chart** (Stacks, "Modules of finite
presentation"). On a trivialising chart `U` — i.e. with an isomorphism
`M|_U ≅ 𝒪_U` — the restriction `M.over U` admits the trivial finite free
presentation (one generator, no relations), obtained by transporting the
canonical presentation of `SheafOfModules.unit` along the trivialisation.

The body bridges the scheme-level restriction `M.restrict U.ι` (carrier of the
trivialisation) with the slice-site restriction `M.over U` (what
`QuasicoherentData` consumes), then transports `unit`'s rank-one free
presentation. -/
noncomputable def IsLocallyTrivial.chartPresentation (M : X.Modules) (U : X.Opens)
    (e : M.restrict U.ι ≅ SheafOfModules.unit (U : Scheme).ringCatSheaf) :
    (M.over U).Presentation :=
  sorry

/-! ## §3. Finite presentation of a locally trivial line bundle -/

/-- **Locally trivial `⟹` finitely presented** (Stacks 0B8M / Lemma 17.25.4).
A locally trivial module on a scheme is finitely presented: assemble a
`QuasicoherentData` from the trivialising affine cover
(`exists_trivializing_cover`), whose presentation at each index is the finite
free presentation of `chartPresentation`, and feed it to
`SheafOfModules.IsFinitePresentation.mk`. No stalk computation or
neighbourhood-spreading is involved. -/
theorem IsLocallyTrivial.isFinitePresentation {M : X.Modules}
    (hM : IsLocallyTrivial M) :
    M.IsFinitePresentation := by
  sorry

/-- **Locally trivial `⟹` finite type and quasi-coherent** (corollary of
`isFinitePresentation`). Finite presentation implies finite type by Mathlib's
`SheafOfModules.instIsFiniteTypeOfIsFinitePresentation` and quasi-coherence by
`SheafOfModules.instIsQuasicoherentOfIsFinitePresentation`. -/
theorem IsLocallyTrivial.isFiniteType {M : X.Modules}
    (hM : IsLocallyTrivial M) :
    M.IsFiniteType := by
  sorry

/-! ## §4. Rank one and flatness record -/

/-- **Chart-local rank one and flatness** (Stacks, "Locally free sheaves":
finite locally free of rank `r`). On a trivialising chart the restriction
`M|_U ≅ 𝒪_U` is free of rank one over `𝒪_U`; in particular flat (`𝒪_U` is a flat
module over itself, and flatness is preserved by the isomorphism). The fact is
recorded chart-locally — as the rank-one free model `M|_U ≅ 𝒪_U = ⊕_{*} 𝒪_U`
the Quot embedding consumes — because Mathlib has no `SheafOfModules`-level
locally-free / flat / rank predicate. -/
theorem IsLocallyTrivial.chart_free_rank_one {M : X.Modules}
    (hM : IsLocallyTrivial M) (x : X) :
    ∃ U : X.Opens, x ∈ U ∧ IsAffineOpen U ∧
      Nonempty (M.restrict U.ι ≅ SheafOfModules.unit (U : Scheme).ringCatSheaf) := by
  sorry

end LineBundle

end Scheme

end AlgebraicGeometry
